title: Benchmark harness measuring framework success
description: Build a repeatable benchmark that runs a defined reference project (spec'd in advance) through agent execution and measures whether this framework earns its overhead. Three measures, per Brooks: (1) total token consumption, (2) % of project completed to spec, (3) wall-clock time. Design should compare scaffolded (to-execution Instance) vs unscaffolded baseline runs on the same spec. Needs its own grill → PRD before implementation.
acceptance_criteria: A reference spec exists against which % completion is mechanically assessable
One command (or documented procedure) produces all three measures for a run
Scaffolded vs unscaffolded comparison is possible on the same spec
notes: Filed by Team Lead from Brooks's directive 2026-06-07, mid-EXEC-001 grill.
